notes: Reproduced as reported. External-Dependency-Info.txt (shipped as THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt in NuGet packages) still lists libmicrohttpd at lines 1475-1988, despite the dependency being commented out in externals/skia/DEPS (line 52). The file has not been updated since its initial commit. This is a stale metadata entry that should be removed.

versionResults.notes: Reporter linked to release/3.119.3-preview.1 branch showing libmicrohttpd at line 1475. The file has not changed since its initial commit, so all released versions contain the stale entry.
Main branch (HEAD) still contains the libmicrohttpd entry at lines 1475-1988 in External-Dependency-Info.txt. The bug persists on main.

errorMessages.primaryError: External-Dependency-Info.txt lists libmicrohttpd (lines 1475-1988) as a third-party dependency despite the DEPS entry being commented out, meaning the dependency is no longer fetched or used. This stale entry ships to all users as THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt in NuGet packages.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this! I was able to confirm that `External-Dependency-Info.txt` still lists `libmicrohttpd` (lines 1475–1988) on the main branch, even though the dependency entry in `externals/skia/DEPS` has been commented out.

This file is shipped as `THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt` in all SkiaSharp NuGet packages, so the stale entry is present for all users.

**Reproduction details:**
- `grep -n 'libmicrohttpd' External-Dependency-Info.txt` shows entries at lines 1475 and 1988
- `externals/skia/DEPS` line 52 has the microhttpd entry commented out with `#`
- `cgmanifest.json` does not reference libmicrohttpd (already cleaned)

The fix is to remove lines 1474–1989 (the entire libmicrohttpd section including separators) from `External-Dependency-Info.txt`.

actionability.reason: The stale libmicrohttpd entry in External-Dependency-Info.txt should be removed. This is a straightforward metadata cleanup with no risk to functionality.

workarounds: No runtime impact — the stale entry is informational only and does not affect SkiaSharp functionality

actions.description: Add label 'area/packaging' to indicate this is a packaging/metadata issue
